purpose: Join key between the TanitEval canonical val build and the val build physically present on tanitad-pod3.
canonical_val_build: physicalai-val-0c5f7dac3b11 (40 episodes, tanitad-eval:/root/valdata) - the corpus every TanitEval/MODEL_REGISTRY number is computed on
pod3_val_build: physicalai-val-f1b378f295ae (80 episodes, tanitad-pod3:/workspace/pai_epcache) - the only val epcache on the GPU that was free
why_they_differ: split_clips() permutes with torch.randperm(len(clips)), so the val draw depends on the clip-count bound. The canonical build came from a 200-clip discovery (160 train / 40 val); pod3's from a 500-clip discovery (400 train / 100 val, 80 materialised). Neither is a subset of the other.
match_method: exact pose fingerprint - the tuple of (t, net_dyaw_deg, arc_m, peak_kappa, clip_len) from refb_labels.route_from_future_v21 over all 5 stride-40 windows of each episode. A collision would require two distinct clips with identical curvature at five offsets.
how_to_use: A label record from this run whose `episode` is a value of map_pod3_to_canonical can be attached directly to the canonical val window of the same `t`. The other 32 canonical episodes are NOT covered by this run.
escalation: Labelling the remaining 32 canonical val episodes needs the canonical epcache on a free GPU pod. pod3 cannot rebuild it: its R0 root /workspace/pai_build holds only 4 discoverable clips (verified via discover_r0_clips), and pods cannot SSH each other. Options: (a) run this harness on tanitad-eval when its sweep finishes, or (b) push the 40-episode epcache (~4.7 GB) eval-pod -> HF -> pod3.
